overview.short: Amaramkavu is a sacred grove and Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess VanaDurga, situated in Kolani, Thodupuzha Municipality, Idukki district, Kerala.
overview.description: Amaramkavu is a sacred grove and Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess VanaDurga, situated in Kolani, Thodupuzha Municipality, Idukki district, Kerala. Amaramkavu is the largest sacred grove in the Idukki district. Amaramkavu has an area near to 3 acres and is rich in biodiversity.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thodupuzha
topPlaces.description: Thodupuzha ([kaʈ:ap:ɐna]) is a municipality and taluk in the Idukki district of Kerala, India. It is located 55 km (34.2 mi) west of the district headquarters in Painavu and about 195 km (121.2 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Kalayanthani
topPlaces.description: Kalayanthani is a small town in Thodupuzha Taluk. It lies in the Idukki district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Perumpillichira
topPlaces.description: Perumpillichira is a small village that comes under the Panchayat of Kumaramangalam in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. The name ‘Perumpillichira’ comes from the name of a large water reservoir adjacent to a Hindu temple at the heart of the village.

topPlaces.name: Ilaveezha Poonchira
topPlaces.description: Ilaveezhapoonchira is a tourist destination located in Melukavu village in Kottayam district near Kanjar. Ilaveezha Poonchira is surrounded by three enchanting hillocks - Mankunnu, Kodayathoormala and Thonippara.

topPlaces.name: Karikkode Bhagavathy Temple
topPlaces.description: Karikkode Bhagavathy Temple (Malayalam: കാരിക്കോട് ഭഗവതി ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple located in the village of Karikkode near Thodupuzha in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. The structure is believed to be 460 years old.

topPlaces.name: Vandamattom
topPlaces.description: Vandamattom is a village in Kodikulam Panchayath, Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Sree Krishna Swami Temple, Thodupuzha
topPlaces.description: Sree Krishna Swami Temple is a Hindu temple situated at the heart of Thodupuzha town in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located on the banks of Thodupuzhayar, a tributary of river Muvattupuzha.

topPlaces.name: Kanjiramattam Sree Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Kanjiramattam Sree Mahadeva Temple, is an ancient Hindu Temple in Idukki district situated on the bank of Thodupuzha River at Kanjiramattam Kara in Thodupuzha Taluk. The temple is located about one and a half kilometers south east of Thodupuzha KSRTC bus stand.

items.note: Thodupuzha ([kaʈ:ap:ɐna]) is a municipality and taluk in the Idukki district of Kerala, India. It is located 55 km (34.2 mi) west of the district headquarters in Painavu and about 195 km (121.2 mi) north of the state capital Thiruvananthapuram.
Kalayanthani is a small town in Thodupuzha Taluk. It lies in the Idukki district of Kerala, India.

Perumpillichira is a small village that comes under the Panchayat of Kumaramangalam in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. The name ‘Perumpillichira’ comes from the name of a large water reservoir adjacent to a Hindu temple at the heart of the village.

Ilaveezhapoonchira is a tourist destination located in Melukavu village in Kottayam district near Kanjar. Ilaveezha Poonchira is surrounded by three enchanting hillocks - Mankunnu, Kodayathoormala and Thonippara.

Karikkode Bhagavathy Temple (Malayalam: കാരിക്കോട് ഭഗവതി ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple located in the village of Karikkode near Thodupuzha in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. The structure is believed to be 460 years old.
Vandamattom is a village in Kodikulam Panchayath, Thodupuzha Taluk, Idukki district, Kerala, India.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Sree Krishna Swami Temple, Thodupuzha & around

items.note: Sree Krishna Swami Temple is a Hindu temple situated at the heart of Thodupuzha town in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is located on the banks of Thodupuzhayar, a tributary of river Muvattupuzha.

Kanjiramattam Sree Mahadeva Temple, is an ancient Hindu Temple in Idukki district situated on the bank of Thodupuzha River at Kanjiramattam Kara in Thodupuzha Taluk. The temple is located about one and a half kilometers south east of Thodupuzha KSRTC bus stand.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amaramkavu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amaramkavu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amaramkavu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amaramkavu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amaramkavu?
faq.a: Stays in Amaramkavu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amaramkavu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thodupuzha, Kalayanthani, Perumpillichira, Ilaveezha Poonchira, Karikkode Bhagavathy Temple.
